mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase discount rates applied to equity cash flows, compressing present values and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of high-rate observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above 4.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending, causing immediate negative equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of cases when UNRATE crosses the 4.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and economic momentum, driving positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in >60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints and reduced corporate output, leading to negative forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines do not correlate with negative 20-day returns when FEDFUNDS is rising, with Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation outpaces the federal funds rate, real rates turn negative, prompting equity market repricing toward negative returns as purchasing power erodes.
failure_condition: Negative real rates consistently coincide with positive 20-day SPX returns in >65% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate income and corporate revenue expectations, generating positive forward equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative rank correlation with 60-day forward returns over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 1.0 percent signals economic contraction, triggering immediate negative risk sentiment and short-term equity sell-offs.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >65% of instances when GDPC1 growth drops below 1.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where industrial production rises while payroll employment falls indicates productivity-driven supply expansion, supporting positive equity returns through margin improvement.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-PAYEMS divergence fails to predict positive 20-day returns with statistical significance at p < 0.10.
